---
title: "About"
about:
  template: solana
toc: false
---

<!-- TODO(Som): replace everything below with your real bio. Suggested shape:
     1. Who you are, in one plain sentence.
     2. What you're working toward (the frontier-AI-lab trajectory) and what you're
        currently teaching yourself — GPU/TPU performance engineering, optimisers for
        large-batch training, RL vs pretraining, large-scale training infrastructure.
     3. What this site is: essays, roughly 80% technical / 20% on AI's economics and
        diffusion, published to keep the thinking honest.
     4. How to reach you (email and/or X), once you've decided what to publish. -->

I'm Som. I'm teaching myself how frontier AI systems are actually built — GPU and TPU
performance engineering, optimisers for large-batch training, reinforcement learning,
and the infrastructure that holds it all together — and writing about it here as I go.

Alongside the technical work, I think about how AI moves through the economy: diffusion,
growth theory, and whether we're at the start of something explosive.

This site is where the thinking gets dragged into the open.
